subject: [PATCH v5 17/36] mm/vmscan: disallow reclaim of private node memory

2026-07-20.patch_summary: This patch prevents the Linux kernel from attempting to reclaim memory on private nodes by adding a filter in the shrink_node function. Private nodes do not support reclaim by default, so this change ensures that reclaimers such as direct and kswapd do not operate on them. The approach taken is to check if a node is private at the common chokepoint for most reclaimers, preventing unnecessary work and potential issues.
